cheat.whyC: Why C and not A? In medieval times the alphabet started from A — the lowest note of the system. Later, when major scale became dominant, C major (no sharps/flats) became the reference point. The letters stayed but the starting point shifted to C.

intRef.b5: Tritone — 6 semitones, devil's interval, maximum tension
intRef.b7: Minor 7th — 10 semitones, bluesy, wants to resolve

character.dorian: Minor with a bright 6th degree. Jazz, funk, blues-rock.
character.phrygian: Dark with Spanish/Eastern flavor. Death metal, flamenco.
character.locrian: Darkest mode. No pure 5th — tritone instead. Unstable, anxious sound.
character.ionian: Standard major scale. Bright, happy sound.
character.aeolian: Standard minor scale. Sad, melancholic sound.
character.mixolydian: Major with flat 7th. Bluesy, rock character.
character.lydian: Major with raised 4th. Dreamy, floating sound.
character.harmonicMinor: Minor with raised 7th. Neoclassical, Eastern motifs.
character.melodicMinor: Minor with raised 6th and 7th. Jazz, fusion.
character.minorPenta: Blues and rock foundation. 5 notes, everything sounds good together.
character.majorPenta: Bright, open sound. Country, pop, rock.
